entry.title: How to fill timesheet through odata service api?
entry.content:
I am building a bot to let user to fill time sheet, I checked the api documentation, I did not find any post call for EmployeeTimeSheet. I found ExternalTimeDate API, through which timesheet data from external system can be inserted to SAP SucessFactors Employee Central(Mentioned in the docs.)I am stuck at this, when I upsert any record, the status of the ExternalTimeData is "ERROR". I did not see it getting reflected in EmployeeTimeSheet also.

I am using sap Sandbox Environment. This is just for PoC.

Please do not mind, If I get anything wrong. Your help would be really appreciated.
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

entry.title: Re: Submissions summary of APJ SME SEEDx Development Challenge 2019
entry.content:
Submission#16 - Online Shopping


Partner: Accelon Technologies Pvt Ltd

Submission Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/sap-visual-enterprise-view-vds-files-from-sdk/qaq-p/13975313

Key words: SAP Business One Partner, ERP for Garment Manufacturers


Short Description:

SAP Business One for HANA is a powerful ERP solution designed to enhance operational efficiency for businesses, including those in the online shopping sector. Offered by an SAP Business One Partner, this solution leverages the speed and intelligence of the SAP HANA platform to streamline inventory management, optimize delivery processes, and enable real-time data analysis. By integrating advanced analytics and IoT capabilities, SAP Business One for HANA ensures accurate stock management, timely order fulfillment, and proactive issue resolution. Partnering with an SAP Business One Partner guarantees a seamless implementation tailored to your business requirements.

entry.title: Re: New MM tables in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
Hello, @mickaelquesnot 

Key Innovations in S/4HANA Materials Management (MM)
Unified Material Document Table

MATDOC:
Consolidates material document header (MKPF) and item (MSEG) data into a single table.
Optimized for faster reporting and analysis, using the "universal journal" concept akin to ACDOCA in Finance.
Reduces redundancy and the need for multiple table joins in inventory processes.
Ensures all related fields for material movements, inventory, and valuation are stored centrally.
Enhanced Pricing Data Structure

PRCD_ELEMENTS:
A central table for pricing elements (discounts, surcharges, taxes) across sales, purchasing, and billing processes.
Simplifies pricing analytics by consolidating data previously dispersed in KONV, KONP, and KONH.
Improves flexibility in developing pricing procedures and custom reports.
Essential for real-time pricing visibility and profitability analysis.
Business Partner Concept

BUT000 and Related Tables:
Integrates vendor (LFA1, LFB1) and customer master data into a unified Business Partner model.
Reduces redundancy and ensures consistent master data maintenance.
Supports streamlined interaction for all roles (customer, vendor, etc.) through a single framework.
Simplified Data Model and Compatibility

Streamlining Tables:
Many legacy tables are replaced with fewer, more efficient structures.
Reduces data storage needs and increases performance, especially for real-time analytics.
Compatibility Views:
Bridges the gap for legacy systems, enabling older programs to access data via mappings to new structures without requiring immediate code changes.   JPMCB Credit Card

Advanced Reporting via CDS Views

Core Data Services (CDS) Views:
Provide a virtual layer over physical tables for simplified and flexible data access.
Enable advanced reporting capabilities without altering underlying data structures.
Facilitate real-time data retrieval and analytics.
Example: Pricing Efficiency with PRCD_ELEMENTS
In traditional SAP ECC:

Pricing conditions were scattered across multiple tables (e.g., KONV, KONP), requiring complex joins for comprehensive analysis.
Extracting pricing data for profitability or sales margin analysis was cumbersome and performance-intensive.
In SAP S/4HANA:

PRCD_ELEMENTS centralizes pricing condition records.
This ensures faster access to pricing components and eliminates inefficiencies in custom reporting and analytics.
Simplified logic enables quicker adjustments to pricing strategies and better alignment with business goals.
Benefits of the New Data Architecture
Performance Gains: Simplified tables reduce database load and increase processing speed for transactions and reports.
Reduced Data Redundancy: Consolidation of tables minimizes duplication, decreasing storage requirements.
Flexibility and Future-Readiness: Compatibility views and CDS ensure a smooth transition for legacy systems and empower modern analytics tools.
SAP S/4HANA's shift in data management aligns with modern business needs for agility, efficiency, and real-time insights, making these innovations pivotal for organizations leveraging SAP.

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: can't start DB in DR after stop it
entry.content:
Hi mohamedkhalid-1,

For the secondary site, you can disable it by following below guide and start it as a "standalone" HANA system. Then you will be able to connect it.
Disabling SAP HANA System Replication

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Revolutionizing Expense Management: SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams, now available in the Microsoft App Store. This powerful integration brings expense management directly into the flow of work, providing employees and approvers with seamless collaboration and enhanced productivity. Here’s a closer look at how this integration is transforming expense management workflows.

Key Features of the SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams:

  1. View, Share, and Approve Expense Reports: Employees can easily view their most recent active expense reports within Microsoft Teams. The list includes key information such as the status of each report. With just one click, employees can access detailed views of their expense reports in SAP Concur, making the process efficient and user-friendly.

    Sharing expense reports with colleagues or approvers has never been easier. Employees can generate a shareable card for any expense report and forward it to a colleague directly within Teams. The recipient, provided they have the necessary permissions and the SAP Concur App enabled, can view the report and take appropriate action.

    Approvers can quickly access their most recent expense reports awaiting approval through the approval queue in Teams. They can review the details, add comments, and approve or send back reports directly within Teams. All comments and actions are tracked in the audit log in SAP Concur, ensuring transparency and compliance.

  2. Streamlined Employee Actions: Employees can view their expense reports and share them without needing to leave Microsoft Teams, allowing them to stay focused and productive within their workflow.

  3. Simplified Approvals: Approvers save time by reviewing and approving expense reports directly in Teams, with no need to switch applications. This integrated process ensures a smooth and efficient workflow.

How It Works:

  • Sign-In and Connect: Once the SAP Concur App is enabled in Teams, users can sign in and connect their Microsoft Teams account with SAP Concur. This ensures that employees can securely retrieve information from SAP Concur.

  • Intuitive Prompts: Users can interact with the app by typing prompts or selecting actions from a list of available prompts. Whether it’s viewing reports, sharing them, or approving them, the app provides clear and simple workflows.

Prerequisites for Using the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams App:

To use the SAP Concur for Microsoft Teams app seamlessly, your organization must:

  • Have a contract with SAP Concur and have the Concur Travel and/or Concur Expense solution enabled.

  • Be eligible to and opted into the new Concur Expense and its new booking experience.

  • Get an Administrator to activate the app within your Microsoft environment.

For more information on how to get started, check the SAP Concur help portal for Microsoft Teams.

The Value of Integration:

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams eliminates the need to switch between applications, saving time and improving focus. By bringing expense management into the Teams environment, employees and approvers can:

  • Improve efficiency by managing expenses within their existing collaboration tools.

  • Enhance transparency with easy sharing and tracking of expense reports.

  • Boost productivity by reducing friction in the expense approval process.

This integration is a game-changer for organizations looking to modernize their expense management practices and empower employees with tools that adapt to their workflows.

Ready to Get Started?

The SAP Concur App for Microsoft Teams is now available in the Microsoft App Store. Enable it today and experience the future of seamless expense management. Together, let’s make expense management smarter, faster, and more collaborative.

entry.title: Re: Video as an attachment in sale order in S4 hana publi...

entry.title: Re: Unable to purchase SAP certification exam
entry.content:
Hello All.
An update to all peers who are looking for SAP certification in India region. Create a new case to SAP learning & Adoption team to process your certification purchase attempt and they will help you.

Create a case using the below link.

https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support

entry.title: Re: How to become a functional consultant? Student trying to become a consultant!
entry.content:
Hi Gokul,

Completing the SAP Learning Journey is a great step for entry-level roles, especially since you already have SAP PP end-user experience. Focus on roles like SAP Functional Consultant (Junior/Trainee) or SAP Support Analyst. Highlight your hands-on SAP knowledge, especially SD, WM, and FI modules, on your resume.

Next Steps:

  1. Hands-On Practice: Access SAP demo systems for real-world experience.
  2. Target Companies: Apply to SAP partner companies (Deloitte, Accenture) or industries like manufacturing, logistics, and retail.
  3. Networking: Join SAP forums, LinkedIn groups, and connect with recruiters or consultants.
  4. Continuous Learning: Focus on functional skills and soft skills; consider advanced certifications later.

Persistence and showcasing your practical knowledge will help secure an entry-level SAP role. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: transport SAC data between prod and dev tenets
entry.content:
Hello, it is quiet simple. The objecty you want to transport exists in the target system with the same name but with differents UID's

You can rename/delete/move the objects in the target system and try one more time.

I hope this helps.

 

Best Regards

SL No Title First Name Last Name Email Address Mobile number Subscription Amount SAPI number Date of payment 1 Mr. xxx xxx xxxx@gmail.com xxxxxxxxxx CER001 21,936.20 SAPIXXXXXXXXXX 29/12/2024

entry.title: How does Optimizer perform the scheduling in SAP TM for freight document?
entry.content:
Hello All Members,

I have a question on the functionality of Scheduling of freight document through Optimizer planning.

I know the Planned Arrival and Planned Departure date is determined through scheduling (Forward and Backward). However, how Optimizer determined that I need to perform forward or backward scheduling?

Could you please help me understand the logic?

Thank you

#SAPTM #Scheduling

entry.title: PO Vendor Name in GRPO Query
entry.content:
Hi All

Please advise how to add Purchase Order Vendor Name to this GRPO Query?

SELECT T0.[CreateDate] as 'GRPO Create Date', T1.[DocEntry] as 'GRPO Doc No.', T0.[DocDate] 'GRPO Date', T0.[DocStatus] as 'Doc Status', T0.[U_BLNO] as 'B/L No.', T0.[NumAtCard] as 'DO No.', T0.[U_CONTREF], T0.[U_WSLIP], T0.[U_SUPWSLIP] as 'Supplier Weighslip No.', T0.[U_RECWSLIP] as 'Receiver Weighslip No.', T0.[CardCode] as 'Vendor Code', T0.[CardName] as 'Vendor Name', T1.[ItemCode], T1.[Dscription], T1.[Quantity] as 'GRPO Qty', T0.[U_PO_VW] as 'Supplier Qty', T0.[U_OTHERWEIGHT] as 'Receiver Qty', T1.[WhsCode], T1.[BaseDocNum] as 'PO No.', T0.[U_LOGREMARKS] as 'Logistics Remarks', T2.[DocDate] as 'GR Date', T2.[DocEntry] as 'GR Doc No.', T2.[Quantity] as 'GR Qty' FROM OPDN T0 LEFT JOIN PDN1 T1 ON T0.DocEntry = T1.DocEntry LEFT JOIN RPD1 T2 ON T1.DocEntry = T2.BaseEntry AND T1.ItemCode = T2.ItemCode WHERE T0.[CardName] = [%0] AND T1.[BaseDocNum] = [%1]

ORDER BY T0.[DocDate], T1.[DocEntry]

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: can't start DB in DR after stop it
entry.content:
Hi Mohamed,

once the primary is down, you need to trigger a takeover on secondary side to be able to use it.

As <sid>adm, you can do this by executing below command:

hdbnsutil -sr_takeover

You can find more information on this here: Perform a Takeover | SAP Help Portal and here How To SAP HANA System Replication

Regards,
Dirk

entry.title: Re: Not getting data in read entity for multiple entities
entry.content:
probably you cannot use the result from previous read in your subsequent read.

when second read is executing, the lt_travel is empty probably.

entry.title: Re: Sales Orders not getting transferred to APO from ECC
entry.content:
Hi Just to add on this issue, writing few things for others.

Make sure Availability check is given 02, KP will work.

Also mainly material should be extended to its storage locations otherwise the sales orders do not CIF in APO.

entry.content:

This is my requests call, since my destination used in BTP was of the type BasicAuthentication. 

requests.post(url_cc, proxies=proxyDict, headers=headers, auth = onpremise_auth, data=file_data).
Any reason for Proxy Authentication failed? Kindly help

entry.title: Cannot view SAP Note/KBA 'Error: You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBA'
entry.content:
Dear PERSON_NAME,
I have difficulties in viewing SAP KBA/Notes. It prompted me that I am not the target audience for the KBA.

Question and Answer:
Q: Does the access/display issue happen to all SAP Notes/KBAs or specific one?
A: Yes

Q: Could you pls provide a sample SAP Note/KBA that you have issue with?
A: KBA DIGITS


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot view SAP Note/KBA 'Error: You are not entitled...
entry.content:
3233516 - Sorry! You are not entitled to access SAP Note/KBA - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

Precondition:

The SAP Notes and KBA are not for SAP Internal.

Syptom:

Your account is not lock and you can't see any of the KBAs and SAP Notes.

Cause: 

When you are seeing the "Sorry! You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBA" message on more than one SAP Note/KBA, then that means the maintenance for your Customer Number is in a terminated status. When that happens SAP Notes/KBAs are not available to be viewed, even within the CCC scenario.

Resolution:

You have to contact your SAP Account Manager or SAP Order Manager for further help on this kind of Maintenance Terminated Problem.

entry.title: Re: Deployment is failed - Execution of task "deploy" on...
entry.content:
follow this to find out the detail of the error

Download the application logs via the dmol command and check them for more information.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing - REST API
entry.content:
Hi,

 

another option to look at is using the Tool MDO: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/insights/tool-mdo

For each MDO an OData V2 and V4 API exists: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/insights/apis

Please note the remark / hint on the limitations of MDOs in the second link.

 

Regards,

Marcel

entry.title: CDS View or API of purchasing price condition data (Table A160/A161 in ECC)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently trying to find a CDS view for the purchasing condition table A160 and A161 to create a report to show the data of these 2 tables in S4 hana public cloud system. The data is created by <MEK1: Create condition records: Purchasing for configurable materials>, the data structure in table A160/A161 is similar to the purchasing info record data in table A017/A018. But I just couldn't find any AXXX table of price condition in S4 hana public cloud.

And we also tried to use Odata API <Technical name: API_PURGPRCGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV>, but we did not see how to get condition record data based on <Supplier/ plant/ purchase organization/ purchasing info record category/ material/ variant condition>.

here is the API reference link: Condition Record for Pricing in Purchasing | SAP Help Portal

Could you guys help if you know of any CDS view or API that i can use to achieve this requirement. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps: build up nested API payload with 1 to many relation (Purchase requisition)
entry.content:
Hi,

first of all happy new year!

In my SAP Build project i have the requirement to create purchase requisitions in S/4HANA Public Cloud. These PR's consists of 1 header, an x number of items and every item has 1 account assignment and 2 texts.

What is the best way to build up the payload for the API? I am able to create a PR with 1 item, but i am struggling to build up a payload for multiple items.

Is this possible with SET_KEY? Or do i need to write Javascript for this? Or is there any other option?

Creating the items 1 by 1 is not an option as there is an impact on the workflow in S4. 

many thanks,

Sanne

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud (Grow) - option to develop simple APPs
entry.content: Hello, thanks for the answer but in S4 Public Cloud you cannot access directly to the system using Eclipse.

entry.title: CCO Coupon cannot remove after add more items. Cancellation even not possible .
entry.content:
Dear experts ,

Greetings for the New year 2025 and wish all of your dreams to come true with in this wonderful year ...!!!

Kindly note that today we have found some issue in one of our client's SAP customer checkout  installation (Version 2.0 FP 17 PL05 and SAP Business One 10 HANA 2111) that is in a usual sale we have been added some items and applied a coupon for them first and then there is another item added due to client request and the already applied coupon automatically added to that item as well . Then we have tried to remove the coupon we had applied before to apply another coupon . But at that time an error is showing in the article type bar area saying,

DELETE on table 'COUPON_ASSIGNMENT' caused a violation of foreign key constraint 'DSCPNSSGNMNTBJCTKY' for key (c4f6b0fa-a614-436a-b400-d817a06bfeb1). The statement has been rolled back.

Here after system could not allowed to post the receipt or cancel . We cannot add a new receipt either .

So could you guys please advice us how we can get rid of this matter at our earliest . 

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Tharindu.

entry.title: SMS text notifications are useless
entry.content:
SMS text notifications are useless

BAD USER EXPERIENCE 

the link doesn’t take to the SAP FOR ME case, but to the user settings alert.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Setup Wizard problem
entry.content:
Hi,

Recently, I configured SAP Business One HANA SP2408 (Security) Hotfix 1 for another customer and used the Windows Setup Wizard for DB upgrade as well without any issues. Since the latest SP2411 version caused errors, I decided to use SP2408 for this setup and faced the same error.

I came across a similar post with same error and referred to a recent comment that resolved the issue:

Failed to connect to SAP B1 HANA server - SAP Community

Here's what worked for me:

  • The Setup Wizard functioned correctly when using a Windows Local account, allowing me to successfully upgrade the database.

Following the suggestion in the linked post, I deleted my Domain account's user profile, which was identified as a Temporary profile. This might have been the root cause of the error. I will check with the client to fix the domain profile issue.

I hope this information helps others facing a similar issue.

https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_PURGPRCGCONDITIONRECORD

entry.title: Steps to install an ADD-ON using SAINT
entry.content:
Hey there! ‌‌‌‌😀👋

If you're looking to install a new add-on, you're likely familiar with the SAINT tool. It’s a tool that helps simplify the process of adding or upgrading a software components in your SAP system.

1. What is SAINT?

SAINT is a tool provided by SAP to help us install new software add-ons or upgrade existing ones.

2. Prerequisites for installation using SAINT

System Compatibility: Make sure th SAP system is up-to-date with the necessary kernel and other components that the add-on might require.
Access to the Add-On: You’ll need to download the software package from the SAP Support Portal.
Permissions: Ensure that you have the necessary authorization to perform the installation. This typically means you need access like SAP_ALL, SAP_NEW or a similar role.
System Backup: It’s always a good idea to take a backup of your system before making any changes, just in case something goes wrong.

3. Steps to Install an Add-On Using SAINT

Step 1: Access the SAINT Tool
1. Log into the SAP system and open the SAINT transaction.
2. This will launch the Software Add-On Installation Tool interface.

Step 2: Download the Add-On
1. Go to the SAP Support Portal (previously SAP Service Marketplace) and download the .SAR file for the add-on you want to install.
2. This file contains all the components of the add-on you need.

Step 3: Upload the Add-On to SAP
1. In the SAINT tool, go to the option Import Software Component and select the downloaded .SAR file.
2. SAINT will unpack the file and prepare it for installation. This may take a few moments, depending on the size of the add-on.

Step 4: Start the Installation Process
1. Once the add-on is imported, click on Start Installation.
2. You’ll be guided through a series of steps. The installation wizard will:
- Check your system’s compatibility with the add-on.
- Show details about the add-on and ask for your confirmation.
- Ask you where you’d like to install the add-on (for example, specific clients).

Step 5: Monitor the Installation
1. The installation will begin and SAINT will display a progress bar.
2. Keep an eye on the process, and if any errors or warnings show up, they’ll appear in the log. These could point to issues like missing dependencies or configuration problems, which you can address as needed.

Step 6: Perform Post-Installation Tasks
1. After the installation finishes, SAINT will let you know if there are any further steps required.
2. These might include running adjustment programs to finalize the setup, updating configurations, or running tasks like client refresh or transport imports.

Step 7: Verify the Installation
1. Once everything is done, it’s time to make sure the add-on is working as expected.
2. You can do this by checking:
- If the add-on appears in the list of installed add-ons (in transaction SAINT).
- If all the expected features and functionality are now available in the system.
- If you receive any error messages when using the new features, indicating that something might have gone wrong.

And that’s it! With just a few steps, you can easily install a new SAP add-on using the SAINT tool. Happy installing!

#SAINTTOOL #SAPADDON #SPAM #SAINT

entry.title: Re: Getting error message: Message no. CO764
entry.content: Hi, if you have found the solution for this issue can please post here, since i am also facing same error while migrating work order.

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud-2308_Income Tax Depreciation-India
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

Can you please check the detailed information, which is available on the SAP Help Portal?

Income Tax Act: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/38c875f128b24a55986d911ea8ff42f8.html?locale=en-US

You can provide any feedback and ask questions on the pages:

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud (Grow) - option to develop simple APPs
entry.content:
In fact, it's more or less clear. With S4 Public Hana Cloud, you cannot develop from S4 Public.
You have access to extensibility package (custom CDS, Custom field...).

If you'd like to go further, you need another option and you have ABAP cloud to develop your own system(s). And you can integrate it using SAP Build Work zone for example.

entry.title: Re: Journal Workflow - Rejects and Redos
entry.content: Hello, Thank you very much for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: Re: trying to upgrade to 2211.28, getting Cannot find CMS...

entry.title: VIEW EXIT Class /SCMTMS/CL_UI_VIEWEXIT_TRQ
entry.content:
Hi All,

While going through the enhancements in system, I can see that for enhancing of handling buttons like 'EDIT' and 'REFRESH', in freight unit we can write the code in the class /SCMTMS/CL_UI_VIEWEXIT_TRQ. But I am not sure how they figured out that the code needs to be written here.

Can you please guide me to the configuration/architecture behind this.

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Re: trying to upgrade to 2211.28, getting Cannot find CMS...
entry.content: I tried adding URL patterns, but session is getting closed, INFO [hybrisHTTP36] [InvalidateSessionAndMarkRequestStrategy] Invalidating stale session. INFO [hybrisHTTP36] [Slf4jAuditableActionHandler] session closed WARN [hybrisHTTP36] [DefaultContextInformationLoader] Cannot find CMSSite associated with current URL (https://localhost:9002/checkout/getShipmethodsforZipcode?zipCode=39502)! WARN [hybrisHTTP32] [DefaultContextInformationLoader] Cannot find CMSSite associated with current URL (https://localhost:9002/checkout/saveShipAddress)!

entry.title: Re: Journal Workflow - Rejects and Redos
entry.content:
Hi rb2020,

Please check the latest documentation for the specific fields (step 7):

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/1f5b2ccb57224ed98e27f10ee22977e7.html?state=DRAFT&version=2408.VAL

Alice

entry.title: Re: CDS View or API of purchasing price condition data (T...
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Thanks for your suggestion，I checked this CDS view before，I think this one is only for table KONH & KONP,  which is the detailed information of condition record, but no material, supplier, purchase organization, plant information. For example, for in ECC we have table A160, we can get the Condition record number based on <material, supplier, purchase organization, plant, variant> from table A160, then we can get the detailed information of condition record based on Condition record number from table KONH/KONP.

Best regards,
Roy.

entry.title: Re: SELECT SINGLE vs. SELECT UP TO 1 ROWS
entry.content:
@horst_keller 

How about a "TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS"-equivalent for Select now, another ~5 Years later? 😉

entry.title: Re: Journal Workflow - Rejects and Redos
entry.content: Hello, I just learned that the documentation was updated to answer the questions raised in this question. I'm therefore closing it.

entry.content: But in my laptop windows 11 I cannot find this service named sap cloud connect. how to find service?

entry.content: Brilliant blog Akshat!

entry.title: Re: But in my laptop windows 11 I cannot find this servic...
entry.content: Okayy 👍🏻

entry.title: Internal Error in MDG CR form
entry.content: In SAP MDG Material extention change request form, material is not activated and there is no explanatory message in the activation log. Only writes "Internal error. Notify your system administrator". What could be the reasons for this error?

entry.title: Re: RAP: Draft do not transfer keyvalue into DB-Table
entry.content:
after checking my behavior def i've found the reason for my case:

...
 mapping for /nnn/chckmsgcfg corresponding;
...

If the field-definition in the db-table is different to the data definitions, the transfer of values doenst not work.

🙈

entry.title: Re: While Running Job for full indexing . i am getting be...
entry.content: If your solr server is using the default one, check https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE/d0224eca81e249cb821f2cdf45a82ace/3118ed49fc6d4e0a9cda900fb51744b9.html?locale=en-US if there is any error for solr server when your commerce is being started. Check the startup log.

entry.title: Re: While Running Job for full indexing . i am getting be...
entry.content: If your solr server is using external one, check https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE/d0224eca81e249cb821f2cdf45a82ace/f64b31a2c8274d3eae8a53abf206f247.html?locale=en-US#using-an-external-standalone-server and see if it's running properly

entry.title: Re: CIM Error in S4 : Your tenant is not trusted
entry.content:
Hello,

please check in https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/Sol_Pack/S4C/Library/Setup/4N6_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf the section 10.2.4 Establish an OpenID Connect Trust.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java) 🎉
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" Automate tasks such as creating AI Core artifacts, configurations, and deployments, executing batch inference jobs, as well as managing Docker registries and object storage for training data. The core module provides tools for workflow and scenario management within SAP AI Core. "

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing,

it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori 2025.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript/TypeScript) 🎉
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" This package is ideal for developers who need direct access to foundation models for inference and embedding requests without the additional layers of templating, content filtering, or data masking provided by the orchestration service. "

 

Such a wonderful sharing, it really help us a lot. 

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Debugger scripting - my first real use
entry.content:
Hey Marko, @MarcoK thanks for your kind feedback!

I do agree that this - commenting one owns blog / "patting my own shoulder" - seem a litte strange.

But indeed, it's something I do:
1. If it was a good read, be it helpful or entertaining, I will say so (even if it was "past me" that wrote it).
2. Those comments might help me to find usefull content again (by going through my profiel and see the list of comments). Would be sad to miss out my own blogs that way.

Best
Joachim

entry.title: Re: Can surveys be added to a program
entry.content: Hi Margaret, did you find a solution on this?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Debugger scripting - my first real use
entry.content: Again, in 2025: A nice blog on how to use ABAP Debugger Script RSTPDA_SCRIPT_STATEMENT_TRACE , to spot the difference in seemingly different data flows! Thanks Joachim! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to get table name for Tables or Table historie...
entry.content:
Hi Sanddeep,

The metric in FRUN System Monitoring is only a numeric value which provides you the number of tables with more than one billion rows.
You can find the relevant SQL statements that are used by the Hostagent to collect the metric data in the KBA 3447912 - Change threshold of HANA Tables or Table histories > 1 billion rows alert

In case you are looking for the exact table names please check the content of table M_CS_TABLES on HANA side. (RECORD_COUNT)
For further details please have a look at https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/20ad60f77519101498ccb610c33c3ca6.html

Best regards
Stefan
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Happy Anniversary, SAP Community! Let's Merge Those Accounts!
entry.content:
Hey @craigcmehil  

> Please note that as of January 26th, 2024 the merge tool is no longer available.

why is that? (How to merge accounts now?)

entry.title: Re: EWM VAS - how come the strange DB model, with client-number in DB tables (/1OM/VASOVASH100) ?
entry.content:
Not important, but I am still courious about this!

Context:

>Fact: EWM VAS-Header, in client 100, you'd have to look at table /1OM/VASOVASH100 , and in in client 125 at you would have to look at /1OM/VASOVASH125 .

>Question: Why was this modeled this way? (What thougths have been put into it, what upsides does it have ?)

entry.title: Re: how to get equipment movement history?
entry.content: equipment history button click it will show you all movement & history of the equipment.

entry.title: ECN API error: Unable to load ECNs of the current instance.

entry.title: SAC Public Dimension for user Group?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Is there a possibility in the SAC to activate Public Dimensions only for a certain group of people? I am thinking of a team, for example. Because I have the case that some data is only relevant for the purchasing department and should actually only be used (seen) by them. 

Thank you very much!
Greetings
Niels

entry.title: MDK FlexibleColumnLayout adjust width
entry.content:
With the FlexibleColumnLayout it is possible to split the screen and show two pages.
Is it possible to adjust the width of the two pages? My goal is that both sides are displayed with the same width.

I am using an iPad and MDK 24.7

entry.title: Re: CAP with Fiori Elements: Configure Multiple Views on List Report Tables
entry.content: One of the best blog with proper steps and clarity by giving the annotation structure. Highly appreciate @Ajit_K_Panda and Thanks as it helped to resolve my issues and got clear understanding.

entry.title: MD_ADD_ELEMENTS - MRP Classic
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a request to include Dependent Requirements (SB), in materials that have a MRP type V2 (reorder point planning). By using the BADI, MD_ADD_ELEMENTS, we can see the requirements in MD04, but when we run the MRP Classic, the SB are ignored in the net calculation.

In include LM61YF0F, form add_sub_mdpsx it checks the type of segments and if it's SB it changes the quantity to 0. It there's any way of including the SB in the new requirement calculations?

Thanks

entry.title: Max Value highlight with Cross Calculation and Calculated Measure
entry.content: I want to highlight the max value for each calculated measure from period Jan to Dec. NOTE: There are no dimensions in the table. Model is created in Datasphere. Thank you

entry.title: Hi Experts,I am getting error with Final settlement date error during CC migration
entry.content:
I am migrating single record through LTMC, to create condition contracts, I gave partial settlement date, delta settlement and final settlement dates also in the TLMC template, but still system gives me the below error. how can we over come this issue.

Thanks in Advance.

Rafiq khan

entry.title: Re: Blend data from to models in one story
entry.content: The page is giving 404 error

entry.title: Universal Allocation on the basis of revenue
entry.content:
Hello ,

I am trying to  do margin analysis overhead allocation by giving variable portion in the rules. It is not fetching the receiver data after doing Test run .I am not able to allocate the cost  on the basis of revenue to the product. Proposed me a solution relevant to this.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Professional - SAP Enterprise Architect - Preparation Tips
entry.content:
Dear all,
Thanks for your comments.
Please consider various free learning journeys offered by SAP Learning.

@rajeshps 
Good starting points would be:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework-foundation
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/applying-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework

Of course, if possible, the course IEA10 will help and provide direct responses by the trainers.

 

@dasusrinivas : the SAP EA Methodology takes TOGAF and TOGAF ADM as starting points. It concretizes TOGAF for a broad set of architecture engagements. If it is a nice fit and helpful for your customers (what we assume and hope) you don't need to evaluate. If you want to adjust the SAP EAF ADM, you might want to go back to the TOGAF ADM for specific phases and check out TOGAF advise/perspective.

 

@SubhranshuSekhar : SAP EAF is compatible with the TOGAF and TOGAF ADM. In general, all The Open Group material is therefore helpful. When you are a member of The Open Group you will always have access to the latest TOGAF material (e.g. TOGAF 10). Having said that, SAP EAF material and practice should be sufficient for the preparation. Please have a look at the latest SAP Learning offerings, e.g. the learning journey given above.

entry.title: Re: Down payment clearing Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere for an Existing CDS View in S/4HANA
entry.content: Great job on this blog! I really appreciate how you’ve broken down complex technical concepts into simple, easy-to-understand language.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any fiel...
entry.content: @SugguSandeep: I get the scenario, however the follow up question would be, what is the purpose of the first error message? And what would be the subsequent logic which you would perform clearing of the message. I am trying to understand what business case you are trying to build here. On the other hand, you can look at highlight changes standard logic to see how standard identifies changed fields during CR processing. But that would have severe performance implications on your application.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Synchronizing Default Values with Set/Get Parameters
entry.content:
Hi Jocelyn,

thank you for this blog, as always it's a pleasure to read them and understand SAP better afterwards.

There is one thing I'm missing in this concept - if the admins want to support Launchpad users by changing their default values later on (for example, because the organization changed and everyone should now use a different purchasing organization or company code), this is technically only possible for classic UIs but not Fioris if the Launchpad has been used for a while already, or did I understand it wrong?

Do you think that's a good candidate for an influence request?

Best regards,

Lisa

entry.title: AIWarmup - Installing Joule with a docker one-liner, then having fun..
entry.content:
Intro

In this AIWarmup, we

  • install Joule on BTP with a Docker one-liner in ~6 minutes
  • modify, explore and deploy Joule examples from github
  • dig deeper into the Joule code
  • code and run Joule Tests
  • enrich a Joule use case by creating a new Spring web-service that we deploying to BTP's Cloud Foundry (again with a one-liner) and use to show employee images.

This AIWarmup and others, are also available as Jupyter Notebooks here. Favourite links for this and other AIWarmups are shared in our crowd-sourced upskilling portal.

Necessary Prerequisites:

You need to have:

  • Installed Joule CLI as described here
  • Installed Docker as described here for Mac and here for Windows
  • Installed Cloud Foundry's CLI, as described here
  • Created a BTP Global account in BTP's Canary - Public Cloud Feature Set B exactly as shown in this 1 minute video
    • The name of your new BTP global account should have letters and numbers only (no '-' '_' etc)
    • It is important you choose the same options as shown in that video. Notably, the following checked (ticked) entities should be checked in yours too.

Part 1: Installing Joule on BTP

  • Personalize this command with your own new BTP Global Account name, and your own email, then run it:

 

docker run -it --rm -e BTPGLOBALACCOUNT=xxx -e BTP_MY_EMAIL=xx.xx@sap.com kenlomax/aiwarmup_toinstalljouleonbtp:v2025a

 

  • The console will respond with "Successfully opened: canary.cli.btp.int.sap/loginxxxxx.. Please continue login in your web browser."  Open that url in a browser to login, which will allow the installation to continue. 
  • This will set up your BTP project to support joule, cloud foundry and other useful things... You can watch the progress in the terminal, and also in the BTP cockpit.
  • If there are errors/infinite waits, it is easiest to justdelete your BTP subaccount and start this step again. 
  • If the installation runs without errors, it should end with a personalized command "joule login ..." that you should copy and run in the next step..  This will be similar to "joule login --apiurl https://xxx.eu12.sapdas.cloud.sap --authurl https://xxx.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com --clientid 'sb-das-designer!xxx' --clientsecret 'xxx' --username xxx.xxx@sap.com --sso-passcode"

Login to Joule

  • Copy and run your personalized "joule login ..." command in your terminal, which will output something like "Temporary Authentication Code (Get one at xxx)":

  • Open the page and follow this clickpath to get your authentication code:

If all goes well, it will log you into joule. To confirm you are logged in, run:

 

joule status

 

which should report "Your are logged in ..." 

That's it!  Now that Joule is deployed and running, let's have some fun with it..

 

Part 2: Using Joule...

Clone the Joule repo "joule-functions-example"

  • Create a github token at https://github.tools.sap/settings/tokens with Repo rights and then export to MY_GITHUB_TOKEN:

 

export MY_GITHUB_TOKEN=xxx

 

  • Clone the "joule-functions-example" repository to (for example) ~/jouledemo2025:

 

mkdir ~/jouledemo2025
cd ~/jouledemo2025
git clone https://$MY_GITHUB_TOKEN@github.tools.sap/DAS-Samples/joule-functions-example.git
cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities

 

  • Familiarise yourself with the code and its respective tutorials:
    • hello_world tutorial at https://github.tools.sap/DAS-Samples/joule-functions-example/blob/main/tutorials/helloworld/step2/index.md
    • weather tutorial at https://github.tools.sap/DAS-Samples/joule-functions-example/blob/main/tutorials/weather/step2/index.md
    • products tutorial at https://github.tools.sap/DAS-Samples/joule-functions-example/blob/main/tutorials/products/step3/index.md
  • search the files for the word "destination:" to observe the destination endpoints that Joule expects to find... and that we will set up next.

Define Destination endpoints

Our Joule code expects the following endpoints to be defined:

  • WEATHER (Details here)
  • ES5 - The SAP Gateway Demo system, for products.. (Details here)

We can do this via the BTP UI, but I find it safer to do so via the command line..  Let's prepare 2 files defining destinations, that we will import...

1) A Weather destination:

 

mkdir -p ~/jouledemo2025/destinations
cat << EOF  > ~/jouledemo2025/destinations/destination_weather.txt
Name=WEATHER
URL=https://api.weather.com/v3
ProxyType=Internet
Type=HTTP
URL.queries.apiKey=c322ef22435d40bfa2ef22435df0bfbe
Authentication=NoAuthentication
EOF

 

2) An ES5 destination 

  • Register an account: Sign up for a demo account on ES5 here.
  • where you can also request to reset the password (if you have forgotten it)
  • then personalize, and create the ES5 destination file with your username and es5 password:

 

cat << EOF > ~/jouledemo2025/destinations/destination_es5.txt
User=xxx
Password=xxx
URL=https://sapes5.sapdevcenter.com/sap/opu/odata/sap
Name=ES5
ProxyType=Internet
Type=HTTP
Authentication=BasicAuthentication
EOF

 

Import the destination files

For convenience(?) the following code block will output a URL where you can import your destination files.

Personalize BTPGLOBALACCOUNT below to equal the name of your BTP Global Account.., then run it..:

 

export BTPGLOBALACCOUNT=20250102b
btp logout
btp login --sso --url https://canary.cli.btp.int.sap --subdomain $BTPGLOBALACCOUNT
export BTP_GLOBAL_ACCOUNT_ID=`btp get accounts/global-account --global-account $BTPGLOBALACCOUNT | grep "global account id" | awk '{print $4;}'` 
export BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID=`btp list accounts/subaccount | grep EU12 | awk '{print $1;}'`
echo "BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID": $BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID
echo "Open this URL to import your destination files: https://canary.cockpit.btp.int.sap/cockpit/#/globalaccount/$BTP_GLOBAL_ACCOUNT_ID/subaccount/$BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID/destinations"
echo "and import the destination files $(pwd)/destination*.txt"

 

This will print the URL of the site you should now open to import your destination files.. Open that URL and import and save your 2 new destination files there:

Compile and deploy the Joule Capabilities

When you compile an application, specify which scenarios it will have:

 

cat << EOF > ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/da.sapdas.yaml
schema_version: 1.0.0
name: airwarmupjoulewithdocker
capabilities:
  - type: local
    folder: ./helloworld
  - type: local
    folder: ./weather
  - type: local
    folder: ./products
  - type: local
    folder: ./employees
EOF

 

We can now deploy our Joule code:

 

joule delete aiwarmupwithjoule
cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities
joule deploy -c -n "aiwarmupwithjoule"

 

Confirm deployment

Use the joule list command to list your deployments:

 

joule list

 

which should show 

 

Digital Assistant Name ID
aiwarmupwithjoule      xxxx

 

Testing your Joule app

Add tests for the weather capability:

 

mkdir -p ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather/tests/features

cat << EOF > ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather/tests/features/example.feature
@weather
Feature: Weather capability with GenAI
    Feature Description    
    Background: Initial state
        Given I log in
        And I start a new conversation   
    Scenario: Munich Weather
        When I say "How is the weather"
        Then response has 1 message
        And message type at index 0 is text
        And first message content contains "Could you please provide the name of the city"
        When I say "Munich"
        Then response has 1 messages
        And response relates to "weather in Munich"
EOF

 

And try it out:

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather
joule test aiwarmupwithjoule

 

You should see something similar to:

See https://wiki.one.int.sap/wiki/display/das/Test+the+Capability for tips and tricks on testing..

Launch Joule

 

joule launch aiwarmupwithjoule

 

This should open the Joule GUI where you can try out commands like:

  • what capabilities do you have
  • what is the weather like in Berlin

Activate Debug Mode

  • It is helpful to peak under the covers. 
  • Widen the UI (to be sure all buttons are shown) and you should then see a debug button in the joule client window
  • Click the debug button to open and explore the paths each joule query takes

Try out some Joule queries

For example:

  • what is the weather like in Berlin
  • show me the products
  • who are my employees
    • Note that this returns blank/broken images of the employees.
    • Trace through the code to figure out why (see the file get_employees.yaml for tips..)

    • We will fix that with a Spring Endpoint 🙂

Part 3: Extend an existing Capability with a new Spring Endpoint

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/
git clone https://github.tools.sap/D061192/aiwarmupspringendpoint.git

 

  • Build the Spring Endpoint:

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/aiwarmupspringendpoint
mvn clean install

 

Confirm the spring endpoint works locally..

 

java -jar target/demo-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar

 

  • Ping the endpoint at
    • localhost:8080 and
    • localhost:8080/get-file?file=1.png
    • and confirm you see valid responses
  • Then ctrl-c out

Push the Spring Endpoint to your Cloud Foundry on BTP

Sign in with the default provider:

 

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu12.hana.ondemand.com --sso

 

Push your Spring endpoint to cloud foundry..

 

cf create-space aiwarmup
cf target -s aiwarmup
cf push

 

Run this block and make the 3 replacements that it outputs:

 

export CF_APP_ENDPOINT=https://`cf apps | grep aiwarmupimages  | awk '{print $5;}'`
echo $CF_APP_ENDPOINT
echo "Modify the 1st image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=1.png"
echo "Modify the 2nd image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=2.png"
echo "Modify the 3rd image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=3.png"

 

Redeploy the joule instance

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities
joule deploy -c -n "aiwarmupwithjoule"

 

Relaunch joule 

 

joule launch aiwarmupwithjoule

 

Restart the Joule conversation to drop any cache:

Try "Tell me about my employees" again, and work through the debug output, and code changes, to see why we now have images appearing.

We collect favourite upskilling material in our crowd-sourced portal at https://enablement2024.a9ab9c7.stage.kyma.ondemand.com/  which you might find helpful.

If you like this sort of blog let me know and I'll post more.

Happy exploring 🙂

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom Client IDs in SAP Document Informatio...
entry.content:
Hi Sundar,

I just created a new trial account and followed your requested steps and it all works fine:

  • Go to swagger
  • Delete the default client
  • Create a new client
  • Go to Document Information Extraction UI
  • Notice that the newly created client is shown in the upper right corner
  • Create a new schema of document type custom and activate it after adding your fields
  • Go to documents and upload a document with your newly cretaed schema

Best regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cockpit free trail account - error "Service"...
entry.content: still the same

entry.title: Dummy Test
entry.content:
Hi All,

Greetings!! Happy New Year!!

Can anyone suggest a Dummy Site which have Real Questions for SAP PS Certification Exam.

Thank you in advance for your Help!!

Thanks & Regards,

Rick

entry.title: SAP CAPM unit testing issue when using Change Log Activation annotation.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

in SAP CAPM application, adapted change log service for field is Active with below annotation and library.

  •  "@cap-js/change-tracking": "^1.0.7",
  • annotateService.Product with {
             isActive @changelog;
         }

While writing a unit test in memory DB, if facing an issue, please help to solve the issue.

console.error
[error] - 500 > {
code: '500',
message: 'no such table: sap_changelog_ChangeLog in:\n' +
'INSERT INTO sap_changelog_ChangeLog (entity,entityKey,serviceEntity,changes) VALUES (?,?,?,?)'
}

Please give suggestion how to handle mock the changelog service or create temp CSV file to avoid error.

 

Sample code :

  it("Create Product OData Service with delegate is not provided", async () => {
    const inputProduct = {
      "externalID": "12345",
      "name": "Project Alpha",
      "productOwner_ID": "101",
      "delegate_ID": null,
      "testCoordinator_ID": "103",
      "testLead_ID": "104"
    };

    const { status, statusText, data } = await POST(
      "/odata/v4/service/Product", inputProduct
    );
    draftId = data.ID;
    expect(status).toBe(201);
    expect(statusText).toBe("Created");
    console.log(draftId)

  });

  it("Activate the draft (SAVE button) ", async () => {
  
      const response = await POST(
        `/odata/v4/service/Product(ID=${draftId},IsActiveEntity=false)/Service.draftActivate`
      );
      expect(response.status).toBe(201);
   
  });
 
Thank & Regards,
Vishnu

entry.title: Customer Specific Price List API Example
entry.content:
Hi - I'm fairly (to say the least!) confused about ByDesign API to create a 'Customer Specific Price List'

This is exactly what I want to do - create a price list for a specific Customer (NOT a Group)

All examples i see (for instance:

https://help.sap.com/doc/a2b4c7ef61d64397b2b1d052082a8a08/2411/en-US/PSM_ISI_R_II_PRC_MANAGE_SPL_IN.html)

are suggesting I need a customer GROUP however I only want to do this for a specific customer.

I can do this in the client ok but can't see how to do this via API call ?

Client:

Any help would be most appreciated !

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any fiel...
entry.content:
@0012anirban 

Soon after changing NAME1 Field - Error Message will be getting, so USER has to upload one document type. Once uploading done error message going to get vanish

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content: Thank you😁

entry.title: Re: New Book: Enterprise Architecture with SAP (SAP-PRESS)
entry.content:
@KamVis : you might want to have a look at the following resources

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/sap-certified-professional-sap-enterprise-architect-preparation-tips/ba-p/284058
  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework-foundation 
  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/applying-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework

entry.title: Re: What is the correct syntax for using the OVER() function?
entry.content: Turns out I was actually asking the wrong question - just needed to put SELECT SUM(Balance) FROM( - rest of SQL) AS DT and that gave me the total.

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel , 

Yes, as of today, it is recommended to have Joule and Work Zone in the same Subaccount. 

Regards,

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @MirAli ,

Our teams are working to get Joule entitlements to the partner account. Hope to get announcements soon on this. 

Regards,

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: How can auto-approval of a form be achieved in SAP Bu...
entry.content:
Dear Narenthiran Annamalai.,

Thank you for reaching out with your query regarding automating the approval process using SAP Build Process Automation. I understand you are experiencing an issue with automatically transitioning a form after a certain period.

To achieve the automation of form approval after three months and subsequent transition to an editable state (Form 2), there are a few steps and considerations you might follow:

1. **Workflow Timers and Conditions:**
- Utilize SAP Build Process Automation's capabilities to implement timers. You can create a time-based condition or scheduled job within your workflow to automatically trigger the transition from Form 1 to Form 2 after the stipulated three-month period has elapsed.

2. **Form Configuration:**
- Ensure that Form 1 fields are set as non-editable during the field configuration. In Form 2, configure the fields to be editable as per your requirements.

3. **Automating Task Completion:**
- If you are encountering an error while updating tasks using Postman or any API, ensure that you have the correct permissions and that your API requests align with the SAP Build Process Automation documentation. It is crucial to have the correct authentication and API endpoint setup.

4. **Error Handling:**
- Carefully review the error message provided when attempting to update the task using Postman for more context. This message can help identify issues related to permissions, incorrect endpoint usage, or other constraints in your request.

5. **Using Script Tasks or Custom Code:**
- If feasible, incorporate script tasks or custom logic within your workflow that automatically processes the form transition after three months, ensuring the workflow complies with your business logic.

6. **Technical Support:**
- If the issue persists and you cannot resolve it through configuration or automation tools, consider reaching out to SAP support or your technical consultant for further technical assistance.

Please let us know if you require any additional guidance or have further questions.
